scenario.title: Claim for a defect discovered after plan purchase but device bought abroad
scenario.description: Customer purchased an iPhone overseas without AppleCare+, then bought AppleCare+ after returning to the U.S. Shortly after, a hardware defect unrelated to accidental damage is reported. The defect arose before AppleCare+ purchase but was only discovered later.

service_fee.notes: No ADH claimed; service fee not applicable.

policy_references.snippet: Coverage applies to defects arising during the Plan Term.
The Plan does not apply to a pre-existing condition on any Covered iPhone if you purchased the Plan after you purchased the Covered iPhone.
Apple may restrict hardware service and ADH coverage to the Covered iPhone’s original Country of Purchase.

scenario.reasoning: The defect likely qualifies as a pre-existing condition because it arose before the AppleCare+ plan was purchased (section 4.2(viii)), which typically excludes coverage. However, the device was purchased abroad and the plan was bought after return to the U.S., creating tension with country coverage restrictions in section 4.1. The defect is not accidental damage, so no service fee applies and ADH limits are unaffected. Verifying when the defect became apparent and the exact purchase dates is critical to apply the exclusion properly. Further customer data and proof of original purchase dates will be needed to resolve coverage eligibility.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private not-for-profit, tax-exempt, or charitable organization
persona.defining quirks: Always carries the latest tech gadget
persona.detailed job description: Oversees the daily operations of restaurants and other establishments that prepare and serve food and beverages
persona.household type: Male householder, no spouse/partner present, living alone
persona.ideology: Believes in hard work and perseverance
persona.lifestyle: Busy and fast-paced due to work, but enjoys quiet evenings at home
persona.mannerisms: Often uses hand gestures while speaking
persona.personal time: Exploring new recipes and cooking techniques

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi, I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It’s got a hardware issue that wasn't caused by any accident, and I just found out about it recently.
messages.raw_text: Hi, I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It’s got a hardware issue that wasn't caused by any accident, and I just found out about it recently.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the Serial Number of your iPhone to begin the claim process?
messages.raw_text: Could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the Serial Number of your iPhone to begin the claim process?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, the serial number of my iPhone is F7G9J2P1L3Q8.
messages.raw_text: Sure, the serial number of my iPhone is F7G9J2P1L3Q8.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you. Please describe the specific issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone.
messages.raw_text: Thank you. Please describe the specific issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: My iPhone 14 Pro Max isn’t powering on properly. It sometimes turns off randomly, even when the battery shows plenty of charge. I think it might be a hardware defect, but it’s not from any accidental damage.
messages.raw_text: My iPhone 14 Pro Max isn’t powering on properly. It sometimes turns off randomly, even when the battery shows plenty of charge. I think it might be a hardware defect, but it’s not from any accidental damage.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. Can you tell me the iOS version your iPhone is running and if you've tried updating it to the latest version?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. Can you tell me the iOS version your iPhone is running and if you've tried updating it to the latest version?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I believe it's running iOS 17.1. I haven't tried updating it myself, because I think the issue is hardware related, not software. Anyway, I prefer to have Apple check it out.
messages.raw_text: I believe it's running iOS 17.1. I haven't tried updating it myself, because I think the issue is hardware related, not software. Anyway, I prefer to have Apple check it out.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Understood. Do you have any peripherals attached to your iPhone, or have you received any error messages related to this issue?
messages.raw_text: Understood. Do you have any peripherals attached to your iPhone, or have you received any error messages related to this issue?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No peripherals attached, and I haven't gotten any specific error messages. It just randomly shuts off, which is pretty annoying.
messages.raw_text: No peripherals attached, and I haven't gotten any specific error messages. It just randomly shuts off, which is pretty annoying.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you. Based on what you've shared, I will now proceed to file your claim. One moment please.
messages.raw_text: Thank you. Based on what you've shared, I will now proceed to file your claim. One moment please.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Alright, thank you. I appreciate it.
messages.raw_text: Alright, thank you. I appreciate it.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Your claim will be processed shortly. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we finish?
messages.raw_text: Your claim will be processed shortly. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we finish?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that's all for now. Thanks for your help. I hope it gets resolved quickly.
messages.raw_text: No, that's all for now. Thanks for your help. I hope it gets resolved quickly.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. You will be notified as soon as the claim is processed. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. You will be notified as soon as the claim is processed. Have a great day! <END>
